description: RVA database for non-exported (internal) X4.exe functions, resolved via base + rva at runtime. Example:  "FunctionName": { "900": { "rva": "0x12345678" } }

steps: 1. Search for string 'virtual_navcontext_macro' — it has a unique reference inside this function (lazy static init)
2. The containing function is CreateDynamicInterior (Controllable::CreateDynamicInterior)
3. Verify: must have exactly 2 callers (MD action handler + one internal), ~928 instructions, 172 basic blocks
4. Verify: references strings 'window' (connection tag search) and 'Controllable::CreateDynamicInterior()' (error messages)
_find_hints.byte_sig_note: First 32 bytes of prologue: mov rax,rsp + save r9/r8/rdx/rcx to home area + push rbp/rbx/rsi/rdi/r12-r15. Stable across minor patches.

steps: 1. Search for string 'Cannot find XML file component macro' — referenced in this function's error path
2. Also references 'Attempting to access macro' for venture extension validation
3. Verify: uses FNV-1a constants 0x811C9DC5 / 0x1000193 for hashing macro name
4. Verify: accesses global macro registry (BST root) — heavy callee count (200+)
verify_strings: Cannot find XML file component macro '%s' in index '%s'
_find_hints.byte_sig_note: Large function (~0x580 bytes) with FNV-1a hash loop. Prologue varies by version.

steps: 1. This function is one of exactly 2 callers of CreateDynamicInterior
2. It is the larger of the two callers (~0xB49 bytes vs ~0x300 for the other)
3. Verify: extracts MD parameters via sub_140C7FDD0 (macro), sub_140C7F290 (enum), sub_140C10740 (seed)
4. Verify: passes 14 arguments to CreateDynamicInterior at a single call site
_find_hints.byte_sig_note: MD action handler class. Size ~0xB49. Prologue may vary.

steps: 1. Called by MD_VariantPropertyEvaluator for property ID 410 (generationseed)
2. Extremely small function: just 'mov rax,[rcx+3C0h]; retn' (2 instructions)
3. Verify: single caller path from the variant property dispatcher
1. This function has 1,901 callers — it is the universal UniverseID to Object* converter
2. Byte signature is distinctive (mask on the jump offset at bytes 15-18)
3. Verify: first instruction checks a2 (UniverseID) for null, jumps if zero
4. Verify: uses a3 as access type bitmask (AND with 0x1FFFFFF)
_find_hints.byte_sig_note: First 16 bytes. Bytes 15-18 are a conditional jump offset that may vary. Mask: FF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF.

steps: 1. Find import PeekMessageW (USER32) — its sole code-caller is the MessagePump
2. MessagePump has exactly one heavy callee (>400 insns, sole caller = WinMain) — that is X4_FrameTick
3. Verify: must call IsGamePaused_0 (named export) and reference QPC error string below
verify_strings: Failed to read system high-performance counter!
_find_hints.byte_sig_note: First 16 bytes (register-save prologue: mov [rsp+10h],rbx + push rbp/rsi/rdi/r12-r15). Mask all FF. Stable across minor patches; re-verify on major engine changes.
